stage: 5.1 Computer Science Knowledge Map + 5.5 initial reference dataset
note: Reference dataset per the roadmap's own instruction: 30-50 topics, to prove the import pipeline before large-scale content creation. Schema matches the documented Stage 5.2 Knowledge Node fields exactly, plus a 'domain' field needed for the domain/category grouping Stage 5.1 asks the map to contain. 'unlocks' is derived from 'prerequisites' (never stored independently) to prevent drift -- same rule as the wider SV-OS knowledge graph.

nodes.title: Programming Fundamentals
nodes.summary: Variables, control flow, and basic problem decomposition — the entry point into all of CS.
resources: Automate the Boring Stuff with Python (free online)
learning_outcomes: Can write a simple program that takes input and produces output using conditionals and loops

nodes.title: Functions & Control Flow
nodes.summary: Functions, scope, and recursion as building blocks for larger programs.
learning_outcomes: Can decompose a problem into functions and trace a simple recursive call

nodes.title: Python Programming
nodes.summary: Python syntax, data types, and idioms — the primary language for the AI Engineer track.
learning_outcomes: Can write idiomatic Python for small scripts and data manipulation

nodes.title: Python Data Structures
nodes.summary: Lists, dicts, sets, and tuples as used in real Python programs.
learning_outcomes: Can choose the right built-in structure for a given problem

nodes.title: NumPy & Array Programming
nodes.summary: Vectorized array operations — the foundation every ML/DL library builds on.
learning_outcomes: Can replace explicit loops with vectorized NumPy operations

nodes.title: Discrete Mathematics
nodes.summary: Sets, logic, and combinatorics underlying all of theoretical CS.
resources: Rosen: Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications
learning_outcomes: Can read and construct a basic mathematical proof

nodes.title: Linear Algebra
nodes.summary: Vectors, matrices, and transformations — required for ML, graphics, and much of applied CS.
learning_outcomes: Can perform and interpret core matrix operations by hand

nodes.title: Calculus Basics
nodes.summary: Derivatives and gradients — the mathematical basis for optimization in ML.
learning_outcomes: Can compute a gradient for a simple multivariate function

nodes.title: Probability & Statistics
nodes.summary: Probability theory and statistical inference — the language every ML model speaks.
learning_outcomes: Can reason about a model's predictions in terms of probability distributions

nodes.title: Arrays & Strings
nodes.summary: The most fundamental data structures, and the base for almost every algorithm.
learning_outcomes: Can solve basic array/string manipulation problems

nodes.title: Linked Lists
nodes.summary: Pointer-based sequential structures underlying more complex data structures.
learning_outcomes: Can implement insert/delete/traverse on a singly linked list

nodes.title: Stacks & Queues
nodes.summary: LIFO/FIFO structures underlying parsing, scheduling, and BFS/DFS.
learning_outcomes: Can choose between a stack and queue for a given problem

nodes.title: Trees & Binary Search Trees
nodes.summary: Hierarchical structures underlying file systems, databases, and compilers.
learning_outcomes: Can implement and traverse a binary search tree

nodes.title: Hash Tables
nodes.summary: Constant-time average lookup structures underlying dictionaries, caches, and indexes.
learning_outcomes: Can implement a hash table with collision handling

nodes.title: Graphs
nodes.summary: The structure underlying networks, dependencies, maps, and social graphs.
learning_outcomes: Can represent a problem as a graph and choose a traversal strategy

nodes.title: Complexity Analysis (Big-O)
nodes.summary: Reasoning about algorithm efficiency — required before any algorithm comparison makes sense.
learning_outcomes: Can derive the Big-O complexity of a given piece of code

nodes.title: Sorting Algorithms
nodes.summary: Comparison and non-comparison sorting — a foundational algorithm family.
learning_outcomes: Can implement and compare at least 3 sorting algorithms

nodes.title: Searching Algorithms
nodes.summary: Binary search and its variants — deceptively easy to get wrong.
learning_outcomes: Can implement binary search correctly, including edge cases

nodes.title: Dynamic Programming
nodes.summary: Solving problems by combining solutions to overlapping subproblems.
learning_outcomes: Can recognize and solve a new DP problem from scratch

nodes.title: Graph Algorithms
nodes.summary: BFS, DFS, shortest paths, and MST — the algorithmic core of the graph structure.
learning_outcomes: Can implement Dijkstra's algorithm from scratch

nodes.title: Digital Logic & Boolean Algebra
nodes.summary: Boolean algebra and logic gates — the foundation of all digital hardware.
resources: Digital Design by M. Morris Mano
learning_outcomes: Can simplify a Boolean expression and map it to gates

nodes.title: Computer Organization & Architecture
nodes.summary: CPU structure, memory hierarchy, and instruction execution.
learning_outcomes: Can trace an instruction through fetch-decode-execute

nodes.title: Operating Systems Fundamentals
nodes.summary: Processes, threads, and the core abstractions every OS provides.
learning_outcomes: Can explain the process lifecycle and basic scheduling

nodes.title: Processes & Memory Management
nodes.summary: Scheduling, virtual memory, and paging.
learning_outcomes: Can compute page faults for a given access trace under a chosen replacement policy

nodes.title: Database Fundamentals & SQL
nodes.summary: Relational modeling and SQL — required for almost every backend and data role.
learning_outcomes: Can write and optimize a moderately complex SQL query

nodes.title: Computer Networks Fundamentals
nodes.summary: How data actually moves between machines.
learning_outcomes: Can explain what happens end-to-end when a browser loads a webpage

nodes.title: Supervised Learning
nodes.summary: Regression and classification — the starting point of applied ML.
learning_outcomes: Can train and evaluate a basic regression/classification model

nodes.title: Unsupervised Learning
nodes.summary: Clustering and dimensionality reduction when there are no labels.
learning_outcomes: Can apply k-means and interpret the result

nodes.title: Model Evaluation
nodes.summary: Metrics, cross-validation, and avoiding the most common ML mistakes.
learning_outcomes: Can choose an appropriate metric for a given ML problem

nodes.title: Neural Network Fundamentals
nodes.summary: Perceptrons, layers, and activation functions.
learning_outcomes: Can explain forward propagation through a simple network

nodes.title: Backpropagation & Optimization
nodes.summary: How neural networks actually learn.
learning_outcomes: Can derive backpropagation for a small network by hand

nodes.title: Convolutional Neural Networks
nodes.summary: The architecture behind most computer vision systems.
learning_outcomes: Can explain why convolutions work better than dense layers for images

nodes.title: Recurrent Networks & Sequence Models
nodes.summary: Modeling sequences before transformers existed.
learning_outcomes: Can explain the vanishing gradient problem in RNNs

nodes.title: Transformers & Attention
nodes.summary: The architecture behind every modern LLM.
resources: Attention Is All You Need (Vaswani et al., 2017)
learning_outcomes: Can implement scaled dot-product attention from scratch

nodes.title: Large Language Model Fundamentals
nodes.summary: How LLMs are trained and used at a systems level.
learning_outcomes: Can explain the difference between pretraining and fine-tuning

nodes.title: Prompt Engineering
nodes.summary: Getting reliable behavior out of an LLM without changing its weights.
learning_outcomes: Can iteratively improve a prompt using few-shot examples

nodes.title: System Design Fundamentals
nodes.summary: Scalability, load balancing, and caching — the core of system design interviews.
learning_outcomes: Can sketch a basic scalable architecture for a simple service

nodes.title: DSA Interview Preparation
nodes.summary: Applying DSA/algorithms knowledge under timed interview conditions.
learning_outcomes: Can solve a medium-difficulty DSA problem within 30 minutes

nodes.title: System Design Interview Prep
nodes.summary: Structuring and communicating a system design answer under interview conditions.
learning_outcomes: Can structure a 45-minute system design interview answer

nodes.title: GATE CS Core Review
nodes.summary: A consolidated review pass across the core GATE CS syllabus (digital logic, OS, DBMS, networks, algorithms).
learning_outcomes: Can solve a mixed GATE-style CS problem set within the time limit

projects.title: Build a CPU Process Scheduler Simulation
Build a To-Do List App with a SQL Database
Build an Image Classifier with a CNN
